sources.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
sources.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
sources.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
sources.population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult acute-sinusitis symptom pattern, reassessment, chronic-rhinosinusitis objective confirmation, modifying factors, polyps, and clinician-reviewed allergy or immune testing. It does not diagnose bacterial infection, recommend antibiotics, or order imaging.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1A–1B — acute rhinosinusitis distinction and clinical diagnosis context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statement 6 — treatment-failure or worsening reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Key action statements 1A–1B — acute rhinosinusitis distinction and clinical diagnosis context
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

sources.issuing_organisation: British Society for Allergy and Clinical Immunology
sources.exact_document_title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
sources.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 9.1–9.8 — allergy testing and investigation context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.
